10-year-old shoot mom over VR headset
Fire crews respond to fire at boarded up building
Milwaukee Dancing Grannies planning return
HS football players gain perspective helping vets
She made a promise to her husband to keep their tree farm going
Almost 200 animals rescued from puppy mill
Water study could delay commercial construction
Girl who called 911 for her father honored
Hospitals becoming a 'dumping ground' for kids
Christmas parade road closures and detours
Cars totaling nearly $200k stolen from dealership
Mother fends off raccoon from daughter
Car chase ends with police-involved shooting
$200,000 worth of supplies donated to PB schools
Family throws surprise birthday party at Eagles tailgate for Vietnam vet
Mbappé anota desde los once pasos y pone el primero de Francia sobre Paraguay
items.summary: Tras una revisión en el VAR el árbitro decidió marcar un penal en favor de Francia, el cual fue cambiado por un gol gracias a la ejecución de Kylian Mbappé, quien llegó a siete anotaciones en la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026.

items.title: Guy Stéphan reveló la gravedad de la lesión de Aurélien Tchouaméni: ¿cuándo vuelve con Francia?
items.summary: El asistente de Deschamps habló de la baja de última hora que sufrió el seleccionado galo previo al duelo ante Paraguay en 8vos; explicó el panorama del mediocampista del Real Madrid, quién lo reemplazará y lo que aportará a "les Bleus" en Filadelfia.

items.title: ¡Impresionante! La ceremonia por la Independencia de Estados Unidos que erizó la piel en el Mundial
items.summary: Con Idina Menzel cantando el himno de Estados Unidos, el coro entonando "América the Beautiful", The Roots con "The Fire" y Miss Pennsylvania 2026, Stephanie Skinner, el estadio de Filadelfia celebró 250 años de la declaración de Independencia del país.

items.title: Koundé sobre el calor: “Va a depender de nosotros darle la vuelta a esto”
items.summary: Koundé considera que el calor obliga a los dos equipos a adaptarse y ganar ventaja al hacer correr al rival. Sobre la estrategia de juego, el lateral explica que Francia tiene que ganar la amplitud del campo y dominar la posesión de balón.

items.title: Paul Pelosi faces hit-and-run charges after hitting a parked car in California
items.summary: Paul Pelosi, Rep. Nancy Pelosi’s husband, is facing hit-and-run charges in Napa County, California, in an incident that occurred on Friday, the Napa County Sheriff’s Office said.

items.title: Eustaquio: “Canadá ya es un país futbolero”
items.summary: Stephen Eustaquio se mostró orgulloso pese a la eliminación de Canadá ante Marruecos. El capitán destacó el torneo de su selección, agradeció el apoyo de la gente y aseguró que el país debe sentirse orgulloso.

items.title: Valdano ve a Paraguay con confianza, aplaude a Cabo Verde y exige goles colombianos | Pasión Mundial
items.summary: Jorge Valdano considera que Paraguay tiene un reto contra una Francia sólida, pero los sudamericanos han aumentado su confianza. Sobre Argentina, afirma que Argentina vio un Cabo Verde heroica y sobre los colombianos, cree que le falta más gol.

items.title: El respeto entre Marruecos y Canadá marcó las Voces del Mundial
items.summary: Jesse Marsch y Stephen Eustáquio destacaron el nivel mostrado por Canadá pese a la eliminación, mientras Azzedine Ounahi reconoció la dificultad del partido y celebró la clasificación de Marruecos a los cuartos de final.

items.title: Maxi Rodríguez: “Creo que el rival de México ahora, espero que no sea México mismo” | Pasión Mundial
items.summary: Carlos Pavón y Guti afirman que México tiene la ventaja de la localidad y la altura. Maxi Rodríguez ve el partido ante Inglaterra parejo, pero advierte que la ansiedad de tener que ganar el partido le puede jugar en contra.

items.title: Dembélé, Mbappé y compañía calientan motores para encarar a Paraguay
items.summary: La Selección de Francia se prepara para un duelo importante en sus aspiraciones al título mundial. Paraguay y Gustavo Alfaro quieren dar otra sorpresa tras echar a Alemania.

items.title: In Mount Rushmore speech, Trump veers from U.S. exceptionalism to warnings about communism
items.summary: President Trump ushered in America's 250th anniversary with a darkly political speech that swerved from the typically apolitical, unifying speeches past presidents have given to mark Independence Day.

items.title: Roque Santa Cruz: “Aquí el que tiene obligación de ganar es Francia” | Enlace Mundial
items.summary: Roque Santa Cruz y Alejandro Bedoya consideran que Paraguay ha venido de menos a más y su mejora demuestra la mentalidad del equipo. Santa Cruz considera que Paraguay no tiene nada que perder, cuando Francia tiene obligación de ganar siendo favorita.

items.title: Jesse Marsch, DT de Canadá: “Fuimos mejores en el juego”
items.summary: Jesse Marsch aseguró que Canadá fue superior en el desarrollo del juego pese a caer 3-0 ante Marruecos. El técnico lamentó la falta de contundencia, elogió el esfuerzo de sus jugadores y afirmó que la afición debe sentirse orgullosa del equipo.

items.title: Female American rower completes record-breaking solo journey
items.summary: A Grand Canyon river rafting guide who aimed to become the first American woman to row across the mid-Pacific solo has completed a record-breaking journey from California to Hawaii

items.title: ¡Rugen los Leones del Atlas! Soufiane Rahimi sentencia el 0-3 de Marruecos sobre Canadá
items.summary: Brahim Díaz asistió al delantero, quien quedó mano a mano frente al arco y sentenció este partido de Octavos de Final de la Copa del Mundo 2026.

items.title: Beyoncé surprises fans with a Fourth of July gift — and teases countdown to ‘B’Day’ reissue
items.summary: As if Taylor Swift’s wedding and the nation’s 250th birthday weren’t reason enough to celebrate, Beyoncé is giving fans an excuse to be drunk in love.

items.title: Washington's July 4 parade is called off. The fireworks are still on
items.summary: Organizers canceled the Independence Day Parade late Friday, citing an extreme heat warning with temperatures expected to reach up to 115 degrees. The evening fireworks display remains scheduled.

items.title: Move over Chutes & Ladders: Schisto & Ladders has educational value plus worms
items.summary: Spread by parasitic worms that can infect via a splash of water, schistosomiasis causes debilitating symptoms. It hits kids hard. A board game teaches how to prevent it.

items.title: Florida beach lightning strike leaves 1 dead, 3 injured ahead of July Fourth weekend
items.summary: A man was killed and three family members were injured Friday after a lightning strike at Fort Myers Beach, Florida.

items.title: Serena Williams withdraws from Wimbledon doubles competition after knee injury
items.summary: Serena Williams is withdrawing from Wimbledon doubles competition after injuring her knee at the tournament earlier this week.

items.title: China's military promotes 2 new generals after anti-corruption purge thins ranks
items.summary: The shake-up is believed to be an effort to ensure the military's loyalty to the Communist Party and its leader, Xi Jinping.

items.title: Clinton, Bush, Obama, Biden Share Message for America at 250
items.summary: Former Presidents Bill Clinton, George W. Bush, Barack Obama and Joe Biden sit down with TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager and share a message of hope and optimism as Americans celebrate the country’s 250th anniversary.

items.title: The best Fourth of July sales to shop right now
items.summary: The best 4th of July sales are happening now. Our editors found worthwhile deals on Apple, Yeti, Medicube and more.

items.title: Swimmer bitten on the foot at New York City beach in a suspected shark attack
items.summary: A swimmer was bitten on the foot in a suspected shark attack at a New York City beach

items.title: Chess federation suspends former world champion who accused Naroditsky of cheating
items.summary: The international governing body for chess has suspended former world champion Vladimir Kramnik for at least a year

items.title: Opinion: America is still a work in progress
items.summary: Poets like Walt Whitman and Langston Hughes have written movingly about America.

items.title: A major Supreme Court ruling, and Taylor Swift wedding excitement: The news quiz
items.summary: An absent congressman returns, Wimbledon welcomes back a great, and Trump’s crypto earnings are revealed.

items.title: 'Cautiously optimistic': Swing voters describe their view of America at 250
items.summary: As America turns 250, voters from our Swing Shift project talk about the state of the country. Their views ranged from "uncertain" and "concerned" to "excited" and "cautiously optimistic."

items.title: Cape Verde's historic World Cup ride ends after pushing Argentina to the brink
items.summary: Cape Verde didn't win a match at the World Cup, and somehow, that didn't seem to matter. The African team's debut on this stage was unforgettable.

items.title: Dayslong funeral for slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei begins in Tehran
items.summary: Khamenei, who ruled Iran for more than three decades, was killed in an airstrike on Feb. 28 at the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.

items.title: Reflections on America's 250th birthday
items.summary: NPR member station reporters across the U.S. asked people how they are thinking about their country on its semiquincentennial.

items.title: 3 dead and others rescued after boat capsizes on Wisconsin lake amid storms
items.summary: Three people died and seven others were rescued after a boat capsized on a Wisconsin lake during a strong storm

items.title: The co-owner of lumber mill has died of injuries from the mill fire and explosion
items.summary: The co-owner of a Maine lumber mill has died from injuries he sustained during a fire and explosion at the facility, bringing to three the number of fatalities

items.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are officially married
items.summary: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift appear to be married as an event at Madison Square Garden is underway. This comes as a sign that read "JUST&T MARRIED!" appeared outside of the venue. NBC News' Emilie Ikeda has more details.

items.title: Argentina, Messi pushed to the brink by Cape Verde and its 40-year-old goalkeeper
items.summary: Since this World Cup began, how many people have learned about Cape Verde, the small nation off the west coast of Africa?

items.title: Trump warns of communist ‘threat’ and touts America’s strength at Mount Rushmore
items.summary: No presidential distinction rivals having one’s likeness carved onto Mt. Rushmore — an honor destined to elude Donald Trump, though his allies have pushed for it.

items.title: Wildfire southwest of Denver forces thousands to evacuate, destroys 160 structures
items.summary: A wildfire burning southwest of Denver forced the evacuation of thousands of residents and destroyed more than 160 structures as erratic winds pushed the blaze across two Colorado counties

items.title: Great Americans: A conversation with Debbie Miller
items.summary: As America prepares to celebrate the country’s 250th birthday, NBC News takes a close-up look at one of the nation’s most iconic landmarks and the person who preserves its history. Tom Llamas introduces us to archaeologist and curator Debbie Miller, the guardian of the Liberty Bell.

items.title: After weeks of speculation, Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wed in New York
items.summary: Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. The wedding bash is expected to last into Saturday morning.

items.title: Investigators find no evidence of engine failure in fiery crash of skydiving plane that killed 12
items.summary: Federal safety investigators say in a new preliminary report that they found no indication that engine failure caused the fiery crash of a plane on a skydiving outing last month in Missouri that killed all 12 people aboard

items.title: Erdogan's warm ties with Trump offer Turkey an edge ahead of NATO summit
items.summary: President Donald Trump has berated and belittled many of the European leaders who are expected to attend the upcoming NATO summit in Turkey

items.title: Louisiana's top court halts the criminal case against the state attorney general
items.summary: Louisiana's Supreme Court has halted for now the criminal case against state Attorney General Liz Murrill

items.title: Mamdani offers a contrast to Trump’s vision for America in a 250th anniversary address
items.summary: The New York mayor gave a solidly pro-immigration speech, flanked by recently naturalized citizens, hours before the president plans to give his own address at Mount Rushmore.

items.title: Tribes hope Farm Bill can feed more people and preserve Indigenous culture
items.summary: Dawn and Cassius Spears would like to expand their farm’s reach beyond their farm stand, but it’s challenging

items.title: Possible super typhoon threatens US Pacific territories still recovering from last storm
items.summary: Residents of U.S. territories in the western Pacific are bracing for a possible super typhoon, just months after the region was hit by the strongest tropical cyclone on Earth this year

items.title: Latin Mass, but faithful to the pope: A Minnesota church shows a different way during schism crisis
items.summary: Priests at the Church of Saint Agnes in St. Paul, Minnesota, celebrate a traditional Latin Rite Mass every Sunday, even though the practice has become a symbol of cultural and political battles among some Catholics

items.title: Man with a Tibetan flag dies after setting himself on fire in front of UN HQ
items.summary: Officials say a man holding a Tibetan flag in front of the United Nations headquarters in New York set himself on fire and died

items.title: US employers pull back on hiring in June amid elevated inflation, global turmoil
items.summary: U.S. employers pulled back on hiring last month and added only 57,000 jobs, less than half the previous month’s total and a sign companies still have a cautious economic outlook

items.title: Tropical storm Douglas forms in Pacific Ocean, no threat to land: Hurricane center
items.summary: Tropical Storm Douglas has formed in the Pacific Ocean

items.title: 16 children rescued from Ohio home were 'almost feral,' authorities say
items.summary: Authorities say 16 children from the same family who were rescued from a run-down home in rural Ohio were living in horrific conditions and were confined to just one room over much of the past four years

items.title: Head of the Chicago Police Department to retire after 3 years in the position
items.summary: The head of the Chicago Police Department has announced that he will retire after a short tenure leading the nation’s second-largest police force

items.title: John Dickinson’s missing 1776 signature haunts his legacy
items.summary: Jane Calvert has spent 25 years championing John Dickinson, a lesser-known Revolutionary War figure

items.title: Extraordinary heat in US Northeast arrives to clash with Fourth of July revelry
items.summary: Multiday warnings of extreme heat have landed in New York, Boston and Philadelphia

items.title: Trump administration sues California, Virginia over new gun laws
items.summary: The Trump administration has sued California and Virginia over new laws in both states that restrict the sale of semiautomatic firearms

items.title: NYC police are bracing for July 4, World Cup action, searing heat -- and maybe a pop star's wedding
items.summary: The New York Police Department is bracing for a slew of massive Independence Day celebrations along with a World Cup match this weekend, scrambling thousands of officers on land, sea and air to handle security measures as an intense heat wave bears dow...

items.title: Democratic congressman asks the CFPB to investigate 'rent now, pay later' companies
items.summary: A Democratic member of Congress is calling for a federal investigation into the "rent now, pay later" industry

items.title: Federal Reserve Chair Warsh emphasizes political independence, signals focus on inflation
items.summary: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that the central bank would remain independent and seek to bring down inflation, likely foreclosing the rate cuts President Trump has sought

items.title: Marine heat wave caused seabird deaths off California. El Nino could worsen die-off
items.summary: Many seabirds are starving to death as a marine heat wave lingers off California and fish seek deeper, cooler waters

items.title: Judge orders Pentagon to lift policy that NYT journalists be accompanied by an escort
items.summary: A federal judge has ordered the Pentagon to lift its requirement that New York Times journalists be accompanied by an escort while in the building

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
